Title: Citramalate synthase yields a biosynthetic pathway for isoleucine and straight- and branched-chain ester formation in ripening apple fruit
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: IPMS/MAM/citramalate synthase family; contains GxGERxG and HxH[DN]D motifs; lacks C-terminal R-region found in IPMS
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Citramalate pathway for isoleucine and ester precursor biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step into citramalate pathway and 1-C α-ketoacid elongation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate and acetyl-CoA to form citramalate; also shows activity with α-ketobutyrate in chain-elongation reactions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Isoleucine is a branched-chain amino acid; volatile esters contribute strongly to apple aroma and sensory quality
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: ripening fruit skin/peel and fruit headspace volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes citramalate accumulation and contributes to isoleucine biosynthesis and formation of straight-chain PROP and branched-chain 2MB esters during apple ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily fruit-specific; strongly induced from ripening onset and remains high through senescence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primarily expressed in ripening fruit; low in leaf, root, and stem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray screening; semiquantitative RT-PCR; recombinant protein enzyme assay and kinetics; feedback inhibition assay; 13C-acetate isotope feeding; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; proteomic peptide detection in apple fruit; allelic association with ester phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; entry-point enzyme for the citramalate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening/senescence at room temperature; likely ethylene-dependent ripening context; 13C-acetate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lines with nonfunctional allele show markedly reduced aroma-related ester production; no other growth tradeoff established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance apple aroma through selection or engineering of functional alleles; favorable to retain/introduce functional MdCMS1 and avoid nonfunctional glutamate387 isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient localization/expression assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Initially annotated as IPMS, but functional analysis demonstrated CMS activity. The study identified two Jonagold alleles, MdCMS_1 and MdCMS_2. MdCMS lacks the regulatory R-region and is not inhibited by branched-chain amino acids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCMS is a fruit-ripening-induced citramalate synthase rather than a canonical IPMS. 2. MdCMS catalyzes formation of citramalate from pyruvate and acetyl-CoA and provides the entry point to a plant citramalate pathway. 3. Isotope labeling, in vivo transient expression, and allele-phenotype association support its causal role in isoleucine, PROP ester, and 2MB ester biosynthesis in apple.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: IPMS/MAM/citramalate synthase family; glutamine387 functional isozyme; lacks R-region
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step and α-ketoacid chain-elongation related branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: pyruvate; acetyl-CoA; also active with α-ketobutyrate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate with acetyl-CoA to form citramalate; shows strongest activity with α-ketobutyrate and pyruvate among tested substrates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to apple aroma volatiles and branched-chain amino acid-related metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: ripening apple fruit; N. benthamiana infiltrated leaves for citramalate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional allele/isozyme that drives citramalate production and supports high 2MB and PROP ester phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In apple fruit during ripening; transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assays; kinetic characterization; BCAA feedback tests; transient expression in N. benthamiana; proteomic detection; natural allelic association
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferable allele for breeding aroma-rich apples and engineering citramalate-derived ester formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains glutamine at amino acid 387; not inhibited by valine, leucine, or isoleucine; low threonine slightly stimulates and high threonine inhibits activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCMS_1 is the active Jonagold CMS allele. 2. It localizes to chloroplasts and accumulates citramalate at high levels upon transient expression in N. benthamiana. 3. Its presence is associated with high branched-chain and propyl ester production in apple germplasm.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: IPMS/MAM/citramalate synthase family; glutamate387 low-activity isozyme
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream variant with near-loss of function
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Same predicted condensation reaction as MdCMS_1 but with near-complete activity loss due to amino acid substitution
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: ripening fruit/headspace; N. benthamiana leaves in transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Near-nonfunctional allele associated with very low 2MB and PROP ester phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed with MdCMS assay mixture in fruit, but low activity explains phenotype differences
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana without strong citramalate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: allele sequencing; recombinant enzyme assay; transient expression; germplasm association
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: loss-of-function variant of key entry enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced desirable aroma volatiles in homozygous glutamate387 lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Allele should be avoided in breeding for strong fruity aroma
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Differs from MdCMS_1 by two nonsynonymous SNPs; glutamate387 is strongly implicated as the cause of near-complete loss of activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCMS_2 is a low-activity/nonfunctional allele of apple CMS. 2. Homozygous glutamate387 lines produce much lower 2MB and PROP esters. 3. Transient expression fails to induce high citramalate accumulation, confirming defective in vivo function.
